## Overview

Blackbaud Grantmaking is one of the longest-established grant-management platforms globally. Supplied by Blackbaud Inc. (US-listed; UK operation via Blackbaud UK), it is used predominantly by foundations, nonprofits and public bodies across the US and UK. In UK Gov it is available on G-Cloud 14 and has a modest UK public-sector footprint. Implementations typically take 3–6 months and require consultant support.

## Products & Services

- **Blackbaud Grantmaking** — application intake, review, award, monitoring, reporting.
- Integration with Blackbaud's broader constituent / CRM / financial suite (less relevant for DfT use case).
- Web applicant portal (YourCause Grants).

## Pricing Model

- Published G-Cloud 14 rate: **£2,565.75 per licence per year** (per the Digital Marketplace listing).
- Typical deployment includes setup + training + configuration charges on top.
- Cost scales linearly with named user licences — can become expensive for programmes with many assessors.

## UK Government Presence

- **G-Cloud 14 listed**: yes — [Blackbaud Grantmaking on Digital Marketplace](https://www.applytosupply.digitalmarketplace.service.gov.uk/g-cloud/services/798791371526456)
- **UK data centres**: yes.
- UK public-sector footprint narrower than Flexi-Grant; majority of installed base is US nonprofit.

## Strengths

- Mature product with long track record.
- Well-understood implementation approach.
- Integrated applicant portal.

## Weaknesses

- Per-licence pricing escalates quickly at programme scale (DfT + 9 MCAs + external reviewers).
- Implementation takes 3–6 months and usually requires Blackbaud-certified consultants.
- Not Green Book / TAG / BCR aware.
- Heavier US orientation; UK-specific compliance features may lag.
- Not GOV.UK Design System native.

## Projects Referenced In

- DfT Transforming City Regions Funding (discovery, 2026-04-20)
